compoundTotal.expr: investment.principal * pow(1 + rateDecimal / investment.compoundFrequency, investment.compoundFrequency * investment.years)

rules.message: Compounding frequency must be greater than 0

props.title: Growth by year
props.subtitle: Simple interest grows linearly while compound returns accelerate.

compound.expr: investment.principal * pow(1 + rateDecimal / investment.compoundFrequency, investment.compoundFrequency * min(1, investment.years))
investment.principal * pow(1 + rateDecimal / investment.compoundFrequency, investment.compoundFrequency * min(2, investment.years))
investment.principal * pow(1 + rateDecimal / investment.compoundFrequency, investment.compoundFrequency * min(3, investment.years))
investment.principal * pow(1 + rateDecimal / investment.compoundFrequency, investment.compoundFrequency * min(4, investment.years))
investment.principal * pow(1 + rateDecimal / investment.compoundFrequency, investment.compoundFrequency * min(5, investment.years))

props.title: How to read this tracker
props.subtitle: Use this panel to explain the numbers quickly.
props.className: rounded-3xl border border-white/10 bg-slate-900/70 p-5 shadow-xl sm:p-6

props.content: Interest is calculated only on the original principal, so each year adds the same fixed amount.
Each compounding cycle adds interest back into the base amount, so future interest is earned on both principal and past interest.
Use this view for savings plans, FD comparisons, lending examples, and quick client-side financial explainers.

props.className: mt-8 rounded-3xl border border-white/10 bg-slate-900/70 p-6 text-center shadow-xl sm:p-8

props.content: The longer the horizon, the bigger the compounding gap.
Current projection: compound returns are ahead by {{formatCurrency(derived.difference)}} on a base amount of {{formatCurrency(investment.principal)}}.
